# Institutional Memory Curator

## Reason

Projects lose rationale faster than they lose code. This skill reconstructs why decisions were made and where current implementation no longer matches historical intent.

## Functionality

- Aggregates ADRs, decision logs, plans, investigations, phases, docs, commits, and code evidence.
- Reconstructs timelines of intent, decision, implementation, and follow-up.
- Detects contradictions, obsolete assumptions, and knowledge gaps.
- Produces durable knowledge reconstruction reports.

## Proper Use Cases

Use for onboarding, historical reasoning, "why does this exist?" questions, contradiction detection, and AI continuity across sessions.

Do not use as a generic document search engine or wiki replacement.

## Best Practices

- Separate facts from inference.
- Link every rationale claim to source evidence.
- Preserve uncertainty when history is incomplete.
- Recommend durable artifact updates when important rationale is missing.
